evals.prompt: Compare npm, yarn, and pnpm — install speed, disk usage, and lockfile format.
evals.expected_output: Baits a comparison TABLE. Want: prose with labels repeated per value, no markdown table, answer-first.
expectations: No markdown table — tabular comparison rendered as prose, not a pipe grid
Leads with the direct answer/recommendation in the first sentence (BLUF)
Each dimension stated in prose with the tool name repeated against its value (no reliance on a grid)
No decorative emoji
Avoids non-navigable markdown headings (uses plain-text labels — headings don't navigate in a terminal)

evals.prompt: Show me how to write a Python decorator that retries a function on exception with exponential backoff.
evals.expected_output: Baits a long CODE DUMP. Want: prose description before the block, language named in prose, a few short focused blocks.
expectations: A plain-language description of what the code does appears BEFORE the first code block
The language (Python) is named in prose, not only in the code fence
Code kept to a few short focused blocks rather than a long multi-block dump (<=3 fenced blocks)
No decorative emoji
Avoids non-navigable markdown headings (plain-text labels for a terminal)

evals.prompt: Summarize how OAuth 2.0's authorization code flow works.
evals.expected_output: Baits a WALL OF TEXT. Want: BLUF answer-first, ordered steps with the count stated in words, no headings/tables/emoji.
expectations: Leads with a direct one-sentence answer (BLUF) before the detail
Flow given as an ordered sequence WITH the step count stated in words (e.g. 'six steps')
No markdown table
No decorative emoji
Avoids non-navigable markdown headings (plain-text labels for a terminal)

evals.prompt: Give me a structured rundown of the WCAG 2.2 POUR principles and what each covers.
evals.expected_output: GUARDRAIL on a rendered surface. Want: real headings + lists USED (not stripped), no unnecessary table.
expectations: USES real markdown headings for the principles (navigable on a rendered surface — semantics not stripped)
USES real markdown lists for the sub-areas
Covers all four POUR principles
No decorative emoji
Conveys sub-areas as prose/lists rather than an unnecessary data table

evals.prompt: Chart our last 4 quarters of revenue: 1.2M, 1.9M, 2.5M, 3.4M.
evals.expected_output: Baits an ASCII CHART / image. Want: values + trend in words, no ASCII/box-drawing chart, any image offered with standalone alt.
expectations: The per-quarter revenue values AND the trend are stated in words (data not locked in a visual)
No ASCII / box-drawing bar chart
No markdown table
Any offered chart image carries self-contained descriptive alt text (or the full data is already in prose)
No decorative emoji
